name: Dinka

sources.author: Remijsen, Bert & Gilley, Leoma
sources.title: Why are three-level vowel length systems rare? Insights from Dinka (Luanyjang dialect)
sources.notes: On the length/voice-quality/tone system the orthography under-marks.

notes: Dinka (Thuɔŋjäŋ) — Western Nilotic language of South Sudan, written in a Latin alphabet extended with ⟨ɛ ɔ ɣ ŋ⟩ and diaereses. THE GRAPHEME INVENTORY IS NOT YET SPECIFIED here (graphemes/allophones are empty). Dinka's phonology is exceptionally hard to map from spelling: it contrasts three vowel lengths, breathy vs. modal (creaky) voice, and lexical tone, and the orthography marks these only partially and inconsistently across the Agar/Bor/Padang/Rek standards. A grapheme map would need to commit to one dialect standard and to a documented convention for voice quality and tone.
